reflection:
the AC trick at 5:40 actually worked. you went from waking at 4:30 thinking that was it, to getting another hour after cosita turned the cool on. and you turned it into a spec for richard. the camping circadian idea is one you've been circling for months and yesterday you actually named the mechanism. that counts.

what hit me reading the rest: your worry dump had one item. the speech. that's three days in a row it's the thing. saturday morning you finally got into writing flow and your own words were 'challenge accepted.' the writing shift is real, but the worry hasn't moved with it. usually that gap, between 'i wrote a lot today' and 'i wont be ready', means the practice loop hasn't started yet. writing it and saying it out loud are two different muscles, and the jokes you're missing live in the second one.

thumb flare came back and you're already debugging it (lemon? something in the food?). annoying, but the rest of the system held, pain 3, anxiety 1, mood and energy at 5. body isn't spiraling, it's just noisy.

one thing today: pick a quiet 20 minutes and read the speech out loud once, even rough. not to fix anything. just to hear where the jokes want to land and where you trip. the jokes only get found in the mouth, not on the page.
opener: the AC trick worked. speech anxiety showing up day three though.
focus_for_today: read the speech out loud once today, even rough. jokes only get found in the mouth.
